MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-08-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Richard H. Self of IMAI, TADLOCK, KEENEY & CORDERY, LLP (415-675-7000), appearing for defendant / moving party Sid E. Parker Boiler Mfg. Company, Inc.
Geoff T. Sloniker of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party David Phillips

RULING:
DEFENDANT SID E. PARKER BOILER MFG. COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Sid E. Parker Boiler Mfg. Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration, defendant's General Order 129 interrogatory responses and Sid Danenhauer's deposition testimony create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (handhole gaskets and cabinet door gaskets) attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's objections to Sid Danenhauer's declaration and attached exhibits are overruled. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration and the deposition testimony of Sid Danenhauer with attached exhibits are overruled. The Court did not consider Charles Ay's declaration in making its decision. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Nicole Hatler of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13730 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Re Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Oct-08-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jacob F. Meli of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-2022), appearing for defendant / moving party Borgwarner Morse Tec Inc., as Successor-by-Merger to Borg-Warner Corporation
Geoff T. Sloniker of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party David Phillips

RULING:
DEFENDANT BORGWARNER MORSE TEC INC. AS SUCCESSOR-BY-MERGER TO BORG-WARNER CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION RE PUNITIVE DAMAGES

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant BorgWarner Morse TEC Inc. as Successor-by-Merger to Borg-Warner Corporation's motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's claim for punitive damages is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff failed to present clear and convincing evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. While plaintiff does not need to prove his case for punitive damages at the summary adjudication stage, the Court takes the higher proof standard into consideration. (See American Airlines, Inc. v. Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 1017; Hoch v. Allied-Signal, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 48.) Defendant's objections to plaintiff's Exhibit H are sustained and its objections to plaintiff's Exhibits B, C, D, E, F and I are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Nicole Hatler of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13730 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-06-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jason Rose of Brayton Purcell (415.898.1555), appearing for plaintff / opposing party David Phillips.
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

RULING:
NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION OF HENNESSY INDUSTRIES, INC. FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

Matter called. There being no appearance by any other party or counsel, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. The testimony of Craig Mountz and the declaration of Rudy Limpert create a triable issue whether defendant is liable for Mr. Phillips's alleged exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant's Ammco brake grinding machine. While such machine itself did not contain asbestos or asbestos-containing component parts, there is a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to Mr. Phillips due to defendant's own product contributing substantially to the harm. (See O'Neil v. Crane Co. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 335, Tellez-Cordova v. Campbell-Hausfeld/Scott Fetzger Co. (2004) 129 Cal.App.4th 577, Sherman v. Hennessy Industries, Inc. (2015) 237 Cal.App.4th 1133.) Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the relevant brake linings were unsafe absent the use of the Ammco machinery. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter not reported. (503/TLJ)
